stack_stability_diagram: The image shows six labeled stacks of equal-size brick blocks.
The diagram shows six labeled brick stacks with center-of-mass markers.
The figure shows six brick stacks, each with a COM marker, projection line, and support bracket.
The visual shows labeled brick stacks with red COM markers and support-footprint brackets.
The image shows several labeled brick stacks for a center-of-mass stability check.

stable_stack_label: Which labeled stack will stay upright because its center-of-mass projection falls within its support base?
Use the red COM marker and dashed projection. Which stack remains stable?
Choose the stack whose center-of-mass projection lands inside the support-footprint bracket.
Which option shows a stack that will not tip over?
From the COM projection and support base, which labeled stack stays upright?
tipping_stack_label: Which labeled stack will tip over because its center-of-mass projection falls outside its support base?
Use the red COM marker and dashed projection. Which stack tips over?
Choose the stack whose center-of-mass projection lands outside the support-footprint bracket.
Which option shows a stack that will not stay upright?
From the COM projection and support base, which labeled stack tips over?
